__fixture_tag__: VENDORED SOURCE SNAPSHOT — frozen citation, not live data

claim.material: natural ruby (corundum Al2O3 doped with Cr3+ at ~ 0.1-1 wt%)
claim.condition: 300 K, 532 nm or 405 nm laser excitation, calibrated spectrometer (resolution <= 0.2 nm), n >= 3 spot acquisitions

source.primary: Sugano S., Tanabe Y., Kamimura H. (1970) — 'Multiplets of Transition-Metal Ions in Crystals', Academic Press
source.secondary: NIST gem identification reference; Forman R.A. et al. (1972) Science 176, 284 (ruby fluorescence pressure standard)
source.citation: NIST / Sugano-Tanabe-Kamimura 1970 — ruby Cr3+ R1 line at 694.3 +/- 0.3 nm, 300 K

tolerance.basis: natural-ruby Cr3+ R1 spread reflecting Cr concentration / strain; calibrated-spectrometer resolution <= 0.2 nm
